elements.sent-2411-001: We define the hypergeometric exponential sum associated to a finite family of representations of a reductive group over a finite field.
elements.sent-2411-002: We introduce the hypergeometric $\ell$-adic sheaf to describe the behavior of the hypergeometric exponential sum.
elements.sent-2411-003: It is a perverse sheaf, and it is the counterpart in characteristic $p$ of the $A$-hypergeometric $\mathcal D$-module introduced by Kapranov.
elements.sent-2411-004: Using the theory of the Fourier transform for vector bundles over a general base developed by Wang, we are able to study the hypergeometric $\ell$-adic sheaf via the hypergeometric $\mathcal D$-module.
elements.sent-2411-005: We apply our results to the estimation of the hypergeometric exponential sum.
elements.sent-2411-006: Let $k$ be a finite field with $q$ elements of characteristic $p$, and let $\ell$ be a prime number distinct from $p$.
elements.sent-2411-007: Fix an algebraic closure $\bar k$ of $k$.

elements.sent-2411-073: The paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-074: In Section 1, we express $\mathrm{Hyp _{\psi, !}$ in terms of the Deligne-Fourier transform.}
elements.sent-2411-075: Motivated by this expression, we introduce hypergeometric sheaves over general base under the so called homogeneity condition, using the theory of the Fourier transform over general base developed by Wang (\cite{Wang ).}
elements.sent-2411-076: Over the field of complex numbers, we also define hypergeometric $\mathcal D$-modules so that the complex hypergeometric sheaves are their solution complexes.
elements.sent-2411-077: In section 2, we calculate the $\mathcal D$-modules involved in the definition of hypergeometric $\mathcal D$-modules.
elements.sent-2411-078: In section 3, under the homogeneity condition, we prove the hypergeometric $\mathcal D$-modules are connections on $\mathbb V^{\mathrm{gen }$ and we estimate their generic ranks.}
elements.sent-2411-079: We use information on the hypergeometric $\mathcal D$-modules over the characteristic 0 ground field $K$ to get results for the hypergeometric sheaves over the field $k$ of characteristic $p$.
elements.sent-2411-080: Then in section 4, we deduce Theorem \ref{mainthm for the general case from the homogeneous case.}
elements.sent-2411-081: In this subsection, the ground field $k$ is of characteristic $p$.
